preFix.title: Lavender and lemon soy wax melts
preFix.subtitle: Scented soy wax melts with lavender and lemon essential oils for use in a wax warmer
preFix.excerpt: Wax melts are placed in an electric or tea-light wax warmer where gentle heat releases the fragrance without a flame in the wax itself. Soy pillar wax sets firmer than container wax and holds its shape as individual melts. Lavender and lemon make a clean, floral-citrus blend. Yield is approximately 8 melts from 100 g of wax.
preFix.sourceNotes: Wax melts: also called wax tarts; scented wax portions placed in a wax warmer where heat (from a tea light below a dish, or from an electric element) melts the wax without a flame in the wax. Soy pillar wax (higher melt point than container wax, typically 57 to 62°C) holds shape better as individual melts than container-blend soy wax. Fragrance load: 10% in soy pillar wax is a reasonable maximum; at 10% the wax holds the fragrance well on the cold throw and releases it steadily when warmed. Lavender EO and lemon EO: both are well-suited to wax melts; lemon evaporates faster, giving a bright opening; lavender is more persistent.

content.text: releases fragrance without a burning wick: the wax warmer heats the melt gently from below until it liquefies and the scent evaporates. Soy pillar wax is used here rather than container-blend wax because it sets firmer and holds shape as individual portions that can be sliced and stored. The

of lavender and lemon is strong: these melts will scent a drawer or airing cupboard simply by being placed inside without heating. Place one or two portions in the warmer dish and heat; replace when the scent is no longer noticeable after 30 minutes of warming.

items.name: Soy wax (pillar blend)
items.prepNote: Pillar-blend soy wax; sets harder than container wax; suitable for freestanding melts

items.name: Lavender essential oil
items.prepNote: 8% of wax weight; floral, persistent base note

items.name: Lemon essential oil
items.prepNote: 2% of wax weight; bright citrus top note; adds lift to the cold throw

content.text: Place the soy pillar wax in the candle pouring pitcher. Set in a pan of barely simmering water. Heat, stirring occasionally, until fully melted and at 75°C. Remove from the heat.

Allow the wax to cool to 65°C. Add the lavender and lemon essential oils and stir for 2 minutes. Pour into the silicone loaf mould in a single layer approximately 2 cm deep. Leave undisturbed for 2 hours until fully set.

Flex the silicone mould to release the wax slab. Slice into individual portions using a sharp knife; squares or rectangles of approximately 10 g each are ideal for a wax warmer dish. Store in an airtight container or sealed bag.

Store the melts in an airtight container away from heat and sunlight. They keep 24 months; lavender holds well in wax, though lemon as a top note may fade after 18 months. Keep sealed until use to preserve the cold throw.

preFix.yieldDescription: Approximately 8 to 10 wax melts (from a silicone loaf mould, sliced into portions)

initialFindings.message: paragraph reads at grade 11.0 (qc threshold: 11) — simplify (target grade 6-8)
